blockingIssues.description: `fleet_status` reports a reachable host as `online` even when repo-state lookups (`getHead`/`getBranch`/`isDirty`) fail, and `fleet_sync` returns the repo HEAD after an rsync-style sync even though that commit hash does not identify the synced content.
`fleet_deactivate` and `fleet_pull` both downgrade post-operation `getHead()` verification failures into successful host results, so required repo-intact / resulting-HEAD reporting can fail silently.
`fleet_rollback` can report `status: "ok"` even when post-checkout HEAD verification fails, so the tool does not reliably verify and return the resulting HEAD per host.

MCP error handling is still incomplete: `fleet_status` collapses connection failures to the generic string `Host unreachable`, and valid JSON that is invalid JSON-RPC (for example `[]` or `{"foo":"bar"}`) can still receive no protocol error response.

The Codex integration evidence only simulates Codex by parsing `.codex/config.toml` and spawning the server directly; it does not verify actual Codex discovery of the config entry or tool visibility on a Codex-owned surface.

appliedUpdates.description: Added MCP architecture notes covering repo-root launch assumptions, the `Runtime.runPromise(runtime)` handler pattern, and explicit stdin-EOF shutdown for `StdioServerTransport` with a live `ManagedRuntime`.
Expanded MCP validation guidance with the real client handshake, malformed JSON-RPC probes, transport-level EOF checks, and Codex-discovery validation requirements.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen required manual verification to use the real MCP handshake (`initialize` -> `notifications/initialized` -> `tools/list`) and also invoke at least one feature-relevant tool; for protocol/error-handling work, explicitly require malformed JSON-RPC probes in addition to plain parse-error checks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for `mcp-server-protocol`, `mcp-drift-rollback-tools`, `mcp-error-handling`, and `mcp-codex-integration` all found that the current skill only requires initialize/tools-list smoke checks, which left tool-specific and invalid-JSON-RPC bugs undiscovered.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Replace the simplified `Effect.runPromise(...)` example with the repository's current live-server pattern that passes `ServerConfig.runtime` into handlers and executes programs via `Runtime.runPromise(runtime)(program)`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `mcp-status-sync-tools` review noted that real handlers now depend on runtime injection in `apps/mcp-server/src/server.ts`, while `.factory/skills/mcp-worker/SKILL.md` still teaches the simplified direct-run pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that workers must report `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` whenever they skip the documented TDD or manual-verification steps, rather than marking the procedure as followed after only running automated tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `mcp-activation-tools` review found the worker implemented code before tests, skipped the required stdio manual verification, and still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true` in the handoff.
